paper_title: A multi-proxy analysis of Late Quaternary ocean and climate variability for the Maldives, Inner Sea

methods.method_name: Radiocarbon Dating and Age Model Compilation
methods.description: This method involves radiocarbon dating using accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) and the compilation of an age model for ocean core samples. It is used to determine the age of sediment layers and to correlate them with known climate events.

steps.description: Accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) radiocarbon dating was carried out at the Beta Analytic Radiocarbon Dating Laboratory on mixed surface-dwelling planktonic foraminifera from 35 cm, 80 cm and 140 cm of depth in core SO236-052-4.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS radiocarbon dating on foraminifera samples
inputs: planktonic foraminifera samples from core SO236-052-4

steps.description: The AMS 14 C ages were corrected for the global reservoir age of 400 years in order to minimize potential errors.
steps.searchable_summary: correct AMS 14 C ages for global reservoir age

steps.description: Converted to calendar years using the radiocarbon calibration program CALIB (version 7.0.4) and the calibration curve Marine13.
steps.searchable_summary: convert radiocarbon ages to calendar years using CALIB

steps.description: Additional age tie points were derived from graphical correlation of the benthic δ 18 O record of core SO236-052-4 with the LR04 standard benthic stack using the software AnalySeries 2.0.
steps.searchable_summary: derive age tie points using graphical correlation with LR04 stack
inputs: benthic δ 18 O record of core SO236-052-4

steps.description: The age model of core M74/4-1143 was revised by graphical correlation with the planktonic δ 18 O record of core SO236-052-4.
steps.searchable_summary: revise age model using graphical correlation with planktonic δ 18 O record
inputs: planktonic δ 18 O record of core SO236-052-4
